displayTitle: The Odometer You Wear to Work

beats.narration: A survey meter protects the crew right now. Personnel monitoring protects you across a whole career, and it is your legal record.

payload.title: WHAT YOUR BODY ACTUALLY GOT
payload.sub: The devices that record your radiation dose, shot by shot.

beats.narration: First, the language. Dose rate is the speedometer, how fast radiation arrives, per hour. Dose is the odometer, your accumulated total.
Your pocket dosimeter is a quartz-fiber electroscope. Charge it to zero; radiation ionizes the chamber, charge bleeds off, and the fiber climbs your dose.

payload.heading: Reading your dose in the field
payload.note: CHARGE TO ZERO → IONIZATION BLEEDS CHARGE → FIBER CLIMBS THE SCALE

beats.narration: The badge is the device of record. Film darkens and develops permanently. A TLD traps energy, then releases light when heated. OSL reads out with light.

payload.heading: The badge: your legal dose history

chips.s: Darkens · permanent, but fogs in heat
Same idea · read out with light

beats.narration: So treat that badge like the legal instrument it is. Same spot daily, facing the work, never shared. Lost or damaged? The RSO, immediately.

payload.sub: The badge is a legal instrument — protect it.
